frameworks.name: distributional reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: learning full value distributions rather than expected values via TD backups

frameworks.name: temporal difference learning
frameworks.one_line: extended with a local update rule avoiding spatial non-locality

frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: linear basis from which the proposed code can be computed

frameworks.name: Laplace transform of future rewards
frameworks.one_line: framework linking temporal discounting to a transform-domain code

core_question: Can distributional reinforcement learning be implemented in neural circuits using only local computations?
key_assumptions: dopamine system implements distributional TD backups
value codes should be computable from each unit's own state alone
reward magnitude, time horizon, and discounting are separable dimensions
manipulations: decomposed value distributions across reward magnitude, time horizon, and temporal discounting
replaced non-local imputation step with local TD update rule
constructed code from ensemble of successor representations with multiple discounts
key_findings: proposed code is representationally powerful and computationally straightforward
decomposition supports a local learning rule without inter-unit state sharing
model-based computations allow immediate adjustments to changing horizons or discount factors
code can be computed linearly from successor representations with multiple temporal discounts
interpretations: distributional RL can be biologically plausible through dimensional decomposition and locality
hippocampal successor representations with multiple discounts may provide the substrate for this code
